theme: drill few boreholes to map a buried sparse seam
title: Prony Query Prospector: Mapping a Buried Sparse Seam from a Few Boreholes
strategies: trivial: guess a single constant term equal to the first reading (matches the evaluator's own baseline, fit_count=1) -> ~0.1

greedy: recognize the setup as textbook Ben-Or-Tiwari/Prony and fit a generic order-t linear recurrence from samples 0..2t-1, ignoring the stated AP exponent structure; correct on LOOSE instances (Q>=2t, enough data) but on TIGHT instances (Q<2t) the missing future samples get silently zero-padded, corrupting the recurrence and recovering the wrong seam

strong: exploit the arithmetic-progression exponent structure -- substitute x->g^x so the t unknown roots collapse onto a 2-parameter family g^a*(g^d)^i; brute-force the small (a,d) hypothesis space, solve a t x t linear system for the coefficients per hypothesis, and use the few leftover samples purely to verify/reject -- recovers the seam exactly even when Q < 2t
invalid: submit more terms than the stated sparsity t allows -- rejected outright on every instance
